hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic interactions at position 5 and introducing hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will improve SSTR2 selectivity by forming unique contacts with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3 and TM5 regions while avoiding off-target SSTR3/5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving good selectivity (Δmargin > 0), but structural quality remains a bottleneck. Tightening structural constraints and focusing mutations at non-pharmacophore positions should improve overall quality and increase the pass rate.

proposed_changes.rationale: Clash filtering is too lenient, allowing candidates with poor structural quality to pass. Tightening the clash threshold will improve structural fidelity.
To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved-pocket contacts at non-pharmacophore positions (1, 2, 5, 6, 11, 12) that are likely responsible for off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.
